Good salary. Good title. Still felt behind. 👋

That's where this account starts.

I'm an engineer who got tired of trading 40+ hours a week for a paycheck that never quite caught up — so I started building on the side with AI tools.

No fake screenshots, no "get rich quick" promises. Just an engineer's honest log of what I'm building, what's working, what's flopping, and the exact tools behind it.

Follow along — new builds every week. 👇

#AIagents #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #TechCareer #FinancialFreedom #SoloFounder #ContentCreator #AIforBusiness

6 months ago I had $0 from AI. Here's what changed. 🧵

Month 1: spent my nights testing every AI tool I could find — after my full day job, with zero income to show for it.

Month 2: first small win. Used AI to write and design content for a side project — earned my first $47.

Then I connected the tools together and built my first automation. Content kept going out even when I wasn't working on it.

That's when it clicked — the real leverage isn't the AI itself, it's the system you build around it.

Now I'm documenting the exact stack here, step by step, so you can skip the months I spent figuring it out alone.

Swipe through the journey, then save this for when you're ready to start. 👉

#AIagents #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #TechCareer #FinancialFreedom #ClaudeAI #Automation #MoneyMindset #SoloFounder

You don't need a SaaS to make money with AI. You need a weekend. 🛠️

Here are 3 digital products you can build, package, and sell — using tools you probably already have:

1️⃣ Niche prompt packs → $9–19 (Gumroad)
2️⃣ Notion dashboards/templates → $15–39 (one-time)
3️⃣ AI-assisted mini guides (PDF) → $9–29 (digital download)

Nobody talks about this kind of compounding. 🔄

Compound interest grows quietly for years before it's visible — most people quit before they ever see it.

Content works the exact same way.

Post #1 might get 12 views. But a year from now, it's still indexed, still searchable, still working — while post #50 gets discovered by people who never saw post #1.

Every piece of content is a permanent asset. Unlike a tweet that dies in 24 hours, a good post just keeps compounding.

I treat my content library the same way I treat my brokerage account: small deposits, don't touch it, let time do the work.

The engineers who "got lucky" with content? They just didn't quit at post #20.

What's stopping you from posting today — tell me below. 👇

#ContentStrategy #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #CompoundInterest #AITools #FinancialFreedom #CreatorEconomy #SideHustle #WealthMindset #SoloFounder

This account posts itself. Here's the exact stack. 🤖

No social media manager, no scheduling tool subscription. Just AI + free infrastructure:

1️⃣ Claude → writes captions, hooks, hashtags, and the carousel copy itself
2️⃣ One HTML/CSS design system → reused for every post, zero manual design
3️⃣ A browser script → renders each slide as a 1080x1080 PNG automatically
4️⃣ A free scheduler → posts to Instagram every Wednesday on autopilot

Monthly cost: $0. Manual work: picking the next topic.

Your salary is one income stream. Here's how to build a second. 🔁

Stream 1: a brokerage account with dividend reinvestment turned on. Every quarter, the payouts buy more shares automatically — no decisions, no logins.

Stream 2: this account. Claude writes the captions, a script renders the carousels, a scheduler posts them. I show up once a week to pick the topic.

Different currencies — capital vs. content — but the same engineering principle: build the system once, automate the output, let compounding do the rest.

Most people treat investing and content creation as two separate hobbies fighting for their evenings. Engineers treat them as two systems running in parallel.

Which stream are you missing — capital or content? 👇

#WealthMindset #PassiveIncome #DividendInvesting #BuildInPublic #CompoundInterest #AIagents #FinancialFreedom #SideHustle #AITools #InvestingTips #TechCareer #SoloFounder

4 tasks worth automating before you scale anything else. 🔄

If you're running any kind of side hustle, these are the first things to take off your plate — all doable in n8n, free to start:

1️⃣ Content repurposing — publish once, auto-format and schedule it across every platform.
2️⃣ Lead capture → welcome — new subscriber gets tagged and onboarded instantly, no manual list work.
3️⃣ Monitoring & alerts — a scheduled check watches prices, keywords, or feeds and pings you only when something changes.
4️⃣ Reporting — pull your numbers from different tools into one weekly digest, automatically.

None of this needs a dev team. Just a free afternoon and the willingness to build the boring stuff once.

Which one would save you the most time this week? 👇

#n8n #Automation #AIagents #BuildInPublic #NoCode #SideHustle #PassiveIncome #AITools #Productivity #TechCareer #WorkSmarter #SoloFounder

Every two weeks, your paycheck hits zero. Then it does it again. 📉

That's not a flaw — that's just what an income statement does. It measures one period, then resets, no matter how good last month was.

Your balance sheet doesn't play by those rules. Assets minus liabilities — it never resets, it just compounds, quietly, while you sleep, while you scroll, while you're at work not thinking about it at all.

Here's the part that gets glossed over: two engineers can have the exact same salary and end up in completely different places. Same income statement, very different balance sheets. One spent it. The other converted it into something that compounds.

Your salary is a number someone else sets. Your net worth is the number that tells the truth about the decisions you're actually making with it.

Swipe through, then ask yourself the only question that matters — is your net worth trending up this year, or just your spending? 👉

#WealthMindset #NetWorth #CompoundInterest #FinancialFreedom #PassiveIncome #MoneyMindset #BuildInPublic #InvestingTips #PersonalFinance #FinancialLiteracy #SoloFounder #TechCareer

Your job pays you for hours. These don't. ⏱️

Stream 1 is capital — dividend ETFs that pay out on a schedule, rain or shine. It's the slowest stream to build, but once it's running it needs zero time from you. Set it, reinvest it, forget it.

Stream 2 is content — every post you publish keeps working long after you hit publish. Unlike your job, it doesn't clock out when you do. A carousel from 6 months ago can still be bringing in followers, leads, or sales today.

Stream 3 is skills — and this is the one most people skip. Package what you already know into a template, a guide, a framework. Build it once, sell it to hundreds.

Different speeds, different mechanics, same underlying pattern: capital compounds money, content compounds attention, skills compound expertise. Stack all three and you've built something your hourly rate never could — income that doesn't need you in the room.

Swipe through, then be honest with yourself about which stream you haven't started yet. 👉

#PassiveIncome #DividendInvesting #CreatorEconomy #WealthMindset #CompoundInterest #FinancialFreedom #BuildInPublic #AITools #SideHustle #InvestingTips #SoloFounder #TechCareer

ChatGPT or Claude for content? Here's the honest answer.

I use both, and they're not interchangeable.

ChatGPT wins on speed — fast first drafts, quick captions, lots of variations when I'm brainstorming.

Claude wins on follow-through — give it 5+ rules for tone and length, and it actually sticks to all of them across a long edit.

Both consumer plans are $20/mo, so the real cost is your time, not your wallet.

If you write for a living, the smart move isn't picking a side. It's knowing which tool to open for which task.

Which one do you reach for first? Drop it in the comments.

#AIagents #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #ClaudeAI #ChatGPT #ContentCreation #AIforWriters #TechWorkerWealth #ProductivityTools #AIWritingTools #SideHustle

"Faceless YouTube" isn't faceless.

Everyone's selling the dream of a channel that runs itself while you sleep.

Here's what's actually true in 2025: AI handles the script draft, the narration, and a good chunk of the visuals.

What it doesn't handle is the editing judgment, the thumbnail testing, and showing up every week when the numbers look flat.

I broke down exactly where AI pulls weight and where it still falls on you — swipe through.

If you've tried AI-assisted video, what part took the longest to get right?

#AIagents #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #YouTubeAutomation #ContentCreator #AIvoice #FaceLessYouTube #SideHustle #TechWorkerWealth #ClaudeAI #ContentStrategy

The 4% rule isn't a law of physics. It's a model — and in 2026 its assumptions are getting picked apart (early retirees with 40+ year horizons may need closer to ×28–33, not ×25).

Here's the engineer's move: when a model breaks, you don't panic. You re-solve it.

Financial independence is just an optimization problem with two inputs:

1. Your FI number = annual spending ÷ your withdrawal rate. Choose your multiple on purpose (25x classic, ~28–33x if you're retiring early).

2. Your savings rate = the one variable you actually control. You can chase raises for years (ceiling, boss, taxes), or move the lever that's 100% yours. Go from 10% → 50% savings and your timeline collapses from ~51 years to ~17. Same income. Different system.

Point your engineer brain at your own finances and "retirement" stops being a someday feeling — it becomes a date you can plan toward.

Save the table so you stop guessing, and follow @alphaengineer for the engineer's approach to building freedom with AI.

👇 Drop your savings rate and I'll give you a rough FI timeline.

#FIRE #FinancialIndependence #RetireEarly #SavingsRate #4PercentRule #FInumber #PersonalFinance #FinancialFreedom #MoneyMindset #EngineerLife #InvestSmart #FIREmovement

You don't need a dev team to build a chatbot. You need your FAQ page.

A "no-code chatbot" is really just your own knowledge wrapped around an AI model that already knows how to talk — feed it your docs, it answers in your voice, 24/7.

The build is 4 steps: pick a builder (Custom GPT, Claude Project, or a free tool like Voiceflow/Botpress), upload your source docs, write the instructions (tone, scope, when to hand off to a human), then test and embed.

It's not replacing your support inbox — it's filtering it. The same 10 questions asked 100 times a week get handled instantly, so what lands in your inbox actually needs a human.

Most people already have the tool: ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro both include this at $20/mo, no extra setup cost.

Swipe for the full build, then save this for your next free afternoon. 👉

#AIagents #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #ChatGPT #ClaudeAI #NoCode #Chatbot #SideHustle #Automation #TechCareer #SoloFounder

Your thumbnail does 80% of the work before anyone watches.

For a faceless channel, there's no face to earn that click — the thumbnail has to do the convincing on its own. Midjourney is built for exactly this.

Three things your prompt needs: --ar 16:9 to match YouTube's format, a clear subject with a style reference ("dramatic studio lighting," "bold comic-book style"), and some negative space so text fits later.

The workflow: generate 4 variations, upscale the strongest one, drop it into Canva to add your title text (Midjourney rarely renders text correctly), then export at 1280x720 and check how it looks shrunk to phone size.

Three principles separate a click from a scroll-past: contrast (bright subject, dark background), emotion (a reaction pulls people in), and a curiosity gap (show the result, let the title fill the rest).

Swipe for the full breakdown, then save this for your next thumbnail. 👉

#AIagents #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #AITools #Midjourney #YouTubeAutomation #FaceLessYouTube #ContentCreator #SideHustle #TechCareer #ClaudeAI #ContentStrategy

TikTok's Creator Fund pays roughly $0.02-$0.04 per 1,000 views. A single brand deal can pay more than a month of most people's take-home pay — for one post.

So why do so many creators still chase the Fund first? Probably because it feels more "passive" — no pitching, no negotiating, just post and get paid.

But here's the question that actually matters: who's setting your rate?

With the Creator Fund, it's the platform's algorithm — and it can change the payout formula whenever it wants, with zero notice to you.

With brand deals, it's you. You set the rate, you negotiate it, and once it's signed it doesn't move.

And here's the bigger one: what survives if your account gets banned tomorrow? The Fund income stops instantly — there's nothing left. But your audience relationships, your rate card, your portfolio of past brand work? Those move with you to whatever comes next.

That's the real difference. The Fund pays you for views. Brand deals pay you for an audience — and only one of those is actually an asset.

Save this, then be honest with yourself: is your content built for the algorithm's payout formula, or built for the audience that's actually yours? 👇

#PassiveIncome #CreatorEconomy #TikTokCreator #ContentCreator #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #FinancialFreedom #WealthMindset #SocialMediaIncome #CreatorFund #BrandDeals #SoloFounder

I timed every minute I spend running this account for a month. Total: 6 minutes a week.

Here's where the other 82 minutes used to go — and what replaced them:

1️⃣ Write the copy: 25 min → 2 min (Claude drafts to my tone rules)
2️⃣ Design the slides: 45 min → 3 min (one template + a script renders all 7 images)
3️⃣ Schedule + post: 8 min → 1 min (a free scheduler queues a week ahead)
4️⃣ Pick the topic: 10 min → 10 min — still mine, and it should be

That's 88 minutes down to 16 minutes per post. Over a year of weekly posts, that's roughly 62 hours back — about a week and a half of actual time.

The pipeline costs $0/month to run. The only thing that didn't get cheaper or faster is the one decision that actually needs a human: what to say.

Save this, then go time your own week. Whatever step takes the longest is probably the one you haven't automated yet.

Follow @alphaengineer for more honest behind-the-scenes breakdowns — what's the slowest part of YOUR workflow? 👇

#BuildInPublic #AIagents #Automation #PassiveIncome #AITools #ClaudeAI #SideHustle #TechCareer #FinancialFreedom #BehindTheScenes #ProductivityTools #SoloFounder

I cancelled 5 paid tools last year. Each one has a free AI alternative that does the same job — and I haven't missed a single one.

Here's the full stack:

1️⃣ Perplexity AI (free) → replaced Semrush research. Cited answers with live sources, no tab-switching spiral. Saves ~$99/mo.

2️⃣ Claude.ai (free) → replaced Jasper AI. Give it tone rules once, it writes to your voice every time — captions, outlines, emails. Saves ~$49/mo.

3️⃣ ElevenLabs (free tier) → replaced Murf AI. 10,000 characters/mo free — enough for YouTube Shorts voiceovers or a podcast intro. Saves ~$29/mo.

4️⃣ Otter.ai (free tier) → replaced Rev transcription. 300 minutes/mo free — auto-transcribes meetings, videos, voice notes. Saves ~$29/mo.

5️⃣ Canva Free → replaced Adobe Express. AI background remover, text effects, resize to any format — no subscription needed. Saves ~$30/mo.

Total: ~$236/mo back. All on free plans. All still running.

The paid versions aren't bad — they're just not necessary when you're starting out. Save this, then audit your own stack: which tool are you paying for that has a free equivalent? 👇

#AITools #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #PassiveIncome #FinancialFreedom #AIagents #FreeTools #SoloFounder #TechCareer #ClaudeAI #Automation #ContentCreator

I don't predict the market. I just show up every month.

That's the entire strategy. It's called dollar-cost averaging — and it's the only investing habit I know that works without being smart, lucky, or early.

Here's the math: $200/mo invested for 10 years at an average 8% return → ~$36K total. You put in $24K. The market added ~$12K you didn't have to earn.

The catch? You have to actually do it every month, even when the market drops — especially when it drops. That's when you're buying more shares for the same $200.

Most people don't. They wait for a 'better time.' The market goes up → they think they missed it. The market goes down → they think it'll go lower. The result: the same $200 sits in a savings account at 0.01% interest.

DCA isn't exciting. It's scheduled. Like a cron job that runs every 1st of the month, regardless of conditions. That's the point.

If you're waiting for the right time to start investing — this is it. Not because the market is at the right price. Because the 'right time' feeling never comes.

Save this, then ask yourself: when did you last schedule an investment? 👇

#WealthMindset #Investing #DollarCostAveraging #FinancialFreedom #PassiveIncome #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #PersonalFinance #AITools #SoloFounder #TechCareer #StockMarket

I've prompted Claude hundreds of times to write content. Most of those early attempts were bad — generic, fluffy, nothing like how I actually write.

Here's what changed: I stopped describing my voice and started showing it.

3 rules that actually work:

1️⃣ Give it examples, not descriptions. Don't say 'write casually.' Paste 2–3 of your own posts and say 'match this tone.' Claude reverse-engineers your style far better from examples than from adjectives. Better yet: open a Claude Project and paste your best posts once — it remembers every session.

2️⃣ Add constraints, not wishes. 'Make it punchy' means nothing. 'Max 15 words per sentence, no fluff words like leverage or unlock, paragraphs max 2 sentences' is specific enough to actually change the output.

3️⃣ Give it a role and an audience. 'You're an engineer who builds passive income. Writing for 25–40 year olds in the US, already working a day job, exploring side income.' Claude calibrates tone, vocabulary, and complexity to that profile.

Combine all three in a single system prompt, save it in a Claude Project, and you stop getting AI-sounding output. You start getting something that sounds like you.

Save this template, then try it on your next piece of content. 👉

#AITools #ClaudeAI #ContentCreation #AIagents #BuildInPublic #SideHustle #SoloFounder #TechCareer #ContentCreator #Automation #FinancialFreedom #AIPrompting

100 followers can make you money. The math is more honest than most people expect.

Affiliate income at 100 followers: probably $0–50/mo. At 1K: maybe $50–300. At 10K: $300–2K+ becomes realistic. These aren't guarantees — they depend heavily on niche trust and what you're linking to.

Your own digital product at 100 followers: same ballpark, maybe $0–200/mo if you launch to a warm audience. At 1K: $200–1K+ is possible. At 10K: the ceiling starts to look very different — $1K–$10K+/mo is where creators with high-trust audiences end up.

The difference is leverage. Affiliate income is renting the outcome. You earn a cut of someone else's product, at someone else's margin, on a commission rate they control. Your own product owns the outcome — the margin, the price, the long-term relationship.

But here's the order that actually works: start with affiliate. It requires no product, tests what your audience actually buys, and earns while you're still building trust. Then pay attention to what they click. Your affiliate data tells you which problem they're willing to pay to solve. That's your product idea.

Affiliate is a probe. Your product is the system.

Save this, then comment where you are right now — affiliate, product, or still figuring it out? 👇

#PassiveIncome #CreatorEconomy #AffiliateMarketing #DigitalProducts #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #FinancialFreedom #ContentCreator #SoloFounder #AITools #WealthMindset #OnlineIncome

This account wrote itself this week. Here's the exact AI stack behind it. 🤖

No social media manager. No design agency. No freelancer. Just a system an engineer built on a Sunday.

3 tools. That's the whole stack:

1️⃣ Claude API — writes the caption, hook, and all 7 carousel slides. Input: topic + brand voice. Output: ready-to-post copy. Cost: ~\.02.

2️⃣ Playwright — renders HTML carousel slides to pixel-perfect 1080x1080 PNGs. No Canva. No exporting. Zero manual design steps.

3️⃣ GitHub Actions — posts to Instagram + Threads every Wednesday 11am ET. Runs in the cloud while I'm at my day job.

The total time cost per post: 20 minutes of setup, once. After that, it runs indefinitely.

This isn't about replacing creativity. It's about not manually doing the same thing every single week. An engineer wouldn't manually deploy code each release — they'd write a CI/CD pipeline. Content is the same pattern.

Build the system. Let it run. Spend your time on the next problem.

Which part would you automate first — writing, designing, or scheduling? Comment below 👇

The first $1 from passive income hits different. Most people never make it. Here's why — and the exact path that fixes it. 💡

Everyone talks about $10K/month. Nobody talks about the 0-to-$1 gap. That gap is where most people give up.

The 3 real blockers:
1️⃣ Waiting until it's perfect (perfect never ships)
2️⃣ Starting with a product (build audience first, sell affiliate while you learn what they buy)
3️⃣ Picking one path and quitting when it doesn't work week 1

The actual sequence that works:
→ Start with affiliate links (zero cost, first dollar can come in days)
→ Watch what your audience clicks — that's your product research
→ Build the digital product they've been asking for
→ Automate the funnel and let it run

The $1 isn't income. It's a prod test. A signal that the system works. Most people never ship the first version — so they never get the signal.

Save this roadmap, then comment: what's stopping your first $1? 👇

#PassiveIncome #SideHustle #DigitalProducts #AffiliateMarketing #BuildInPublic #FinancialFreedom #AITools #EngineerLife #SoloFounder #OnlineIncome #CreatorEconomy #WealthMindset

I stopped comparing salaries and started comparing net worth. Here's what changed. 📊

Your salary is a flow. Your net worth is the score. Most people spend their entire career optimizing the wrong number.

The formula is simple:
Assets − Liabilities = Net Worth

What counts as assets: investments, ETFs, real estate equity, cash, business value
What counts as liabilities: mortgage, car loan, credit card debt

Same $120K salary. 10 years later: one person is still salary-dependent, the other has options. The difference? One tracked take-home. The other tracked the balance sheet.

Engineers have a real edge here:
✅ High income floor = more to invest each month
✅ Automation skills = income streams that earn while offline
✅ Compounding leverage = savings rate × consistency × time

The bottleneck isn't the salary. It's not optimizing the balance sheet.

Calculate your net worth today. It takes 10 minutes. That number is your actual score — not what HR deposits twice a month.

Comment your number (or just 'first time calculating') below 👇

#WealthMindset #NetWorth #FinancialFreedom #PersonalFinance #EngineerLife #Investing #ETF #BuildWealth #SideHustle #PassiveIncome #AITools #FinancialIndependence
